title: Coating of a scale inhibitor with a hydrophobic agent for controlled release
abstract: Solid scale inhibitors that are coated with a hydrophobic coating agent selected from the group consisting of: organosilanes, organosiloxanes, polysiloxanes, amine functionalized polyolefins, polymerizable natural oils and polyethylene-vinyl acetates, for controlling the release of the inhibitors into surrounding fluid, providing a slow release that promotes the long-lasting effect of inhibition. The coated scale inhibitors have use in oilfield applications such as hydraulic fracturing operations, gravel pack operations, and in water treatment processes in other industries.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A delayed-release scale inhibitor composition, the composition comprising: a scale inhibitor composition; and a hydrophobic coating disposed on a surface of the scale inhibitor composition. 2. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a scale inhibitor absorbed onto an absorbent. 3. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a mixture of a scale inhibitor and a polyvalent salt. 4. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a mixture of a scale inhibitor and an anti-caking agent. 5. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a phosphonic-based scale inhibitor. 6. The composition of claim 5, wherein the phosphonic-based scale inhibitor is diethylene triamine penta (methylene phosphonic acid) (DTPMP). 7. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a polyacrylate-based scale inhibitor. 8. The composition of claim 7, wherein the polyacrylate-based scale inhibitor is a polyacrylate sodium salt. 9. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the hydrophobic coating is selected from the group consisting of organosilanes, organosiloxanes, polysiloxanes, and any combination thereof. 10. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the hydrophobic coating is a polyvinyl acetate or copolymer thereof. 1 1. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the hydrophobic coating is an amine functionalized polyolefin. 12. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the hydrophobic coating is a polymerized natural oil. 13. A method for the hydraulic fracturing of a formation, the method comprising: combining a composition according to any one of claims 1 -12 with a fracturing fluid to form a hydraulic fracturing fluid; and injecting the hydraulic fracturing fluid into a formation. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the fracturing fluid is a viscosified water-based fracturing fluid. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the fracturing fluid is a non-viscosified water-based fracturing fluid. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the hydraulic fracturing fluid is injected into the formation at a flow rate of between about 60 and about 100 barrels per minute (bpm). 17. A method of making a delayed-release scale inhibitor composition, the method comprising: providing a scale inhibitor composition; and applying a hydrophobic coating agent on a surface of the scale inhibitor composition. 18. The composition of claim 17, wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a scale inhibitor absorbed onto an absorbent. 19. The composition of claim 17, wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a mixture of a scale inhibitor and a polyvalent salt. 20. The composition of claim 17, wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a mixture of a scale inhibitor and an anti-caking agent. 21. The composition of claim 17, wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a phosphonic-based scale inhibitor. 22. The composition of claim 21 , wherein the phosphonic-based scale inhibitor is diethylene triamine penta (methylene phosphonic acid) (DTPMP). 23. The composition of claim 17, wherein the scale inhibitor composition comprises a polyacrylate-based scale inhibitor. 24. The composition of claim 23, wherein the polyacrylate-based scale inhibitor is a polyacrylate sodium salt. 25. The composition of claim 17, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is selected from the group consisting of organosilanes, organosiloxanes, polysiloxanes, and any combination thereof. 26. The composition of claim 17, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is a polyvinyl acetate or copolymer thereof. 27. The composition of claim 17, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is an amine functionalized polyolefin. 28. The composition of claim 17, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is a polymerizable natural oil. 29. The method of any one of claims 17-28, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is placed in an oil prior to application on the surface of the scale inhibitor composition. 30. The method of any one of claims 17-28, wherein the hydrophobic coating agent is placed in a liquid medium prior to application on the surface of the scale inhibitor composition.
